title: BottomSheet — Slide-Up Modal for Confirmations and Input
problem: Destructive and important actions (freeze, delete, unfreeze, leave band, stage change) need a deliberate user confirmation step. Inline buttons can be accidentally tapped; a slide-up sheet forces a conscious second action and provides space for consequence copy.
analysis: Fixed-position overlay with dark backdrop (rgba(0,0,0,0.6)). Sheet panel slides up from the bottom on mount (CSS translateY transition, 200ms ease-out). Content area: white/elevated rounded-t-2xl, safe-area-inset-bottom padding. Dismiss via: backdrop tap, swipe-down gesture, programmatic close, or Cancel button. Trap focus inside sheet when open (accessibility). Scroll lock on body while open. Children are passed as React nodes — sheet is a generic container.
successCriteria:
- Sheet slides up from bottom on open, slides down on close
- Backdrop tap dismisses sheet
- Body scroll locked while sheet is open
- Focus trapped inside sheet (Tab cycles within sheet children)
- Cancel button always present at bottom of sheet content
- Swipe-down gesture dismisses
- z-index above all page content and tab bar
implementation: components/ui/BottomSheet.tsx: `open` + `onClose` props. Uses React portal (document.body). CSS transition on transform: translateY(0) ↔ translateY(100%). useEffect adds/removes `overflow:hidden` on document.body. useFocusTrap hook (tab/shift-tab cycle inside sheet). Children slot for arbitrary content.

decisions.decision: All actions that mutate data open a BottomSheet for confirmation
decisions.rationale: Irreversible operations (freeze, delete, cleanup, unfreeze) need a deliberate second step. Bottom sheets allow showing contextual consequences (e.g., 'What gets deleted / What stays') that inline buttons cannot accommodate.
decisions.recommendation: Every action that mutates data uses BottomSheet. Sheets contain consequence text + danger Button + ghost Cancel.

knownLimitations: Nested sheets are not supported — opening a sheet from within another sheet will break focus trap
Swipe-down gesture uses touch events only — no mouse drag to dismiss on desktop
userGuide: Bottom sheets appear as a slide-up panel when you tap a destructive or important action. Tap outside the sheet or swipe it down to cancel. Tap Cancel inside the sheet to close without taking action.
